repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
It's disabled by default. Enable with

```
cmake -S cmake.deps -B .deps -D ENABLE_GHOSTTY=ON
cmake --build .deps
cmake -B build -D ENABLE_GHOSSTY=ON
cmake --build build
```

comment_data.body: Need to also bump the `cmake_minimum_required()` calls in our `CMakeLists.txt`.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -29,8 +29,8 @@ jobs:
     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
     timeout-minutes: 15
     env:
-      CMAKE_URL: 'https://cmake.org/files/v3.16/cmake-3.16.0-Linux-x86_64.sh'
-      CMAKE_VERSION: '3.16.0'
+      CMAKE_URL: 'https://cmake.org/files/v3.19/cmake-3.19.0-Linux-x86_64.sh'

comment_data.body:
credential persistence through GitHub Actions artifacts

[Show more details](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/security/code-scanning/82)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -90,3 +90,18 @@
 
       - name: Build
         run: make CMAKE_FLAGS="-D CI_BUILD=ON"
+
+  wasmtime:
+    strategy:
+      fail-fast: false
+      matrix:
+        test: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
+    runs-on: ${{ matrix.test }}
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6

@@ -90,3 +90,18 @@ jobs:
 
       - name: Build
         run: make CMAKE_FLAGS="-D CI_BUILD=ON"
+
+  wasmtime:

comment_regular_data.body: Looks like the error are because we need dependencies to be static libraries and ghostty only allows building shared libs at the moment. Either send a fix to ghostty or just ignore the error for now if the idea is to just play around with this.
Merged, update to that commit at least, and the static version is `ghostty-vt-static`in CMakeland.
Note that this raises the minimum CMake version to 3.19.0

Windows be like

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5ef44b93-954f-49aa-a0a3-279842c57b38)

(Windows is the only platform where `GHOSTTY_VT_SONAME` isn't set?)

> Note that this raises the minimum CMake version to 3.19.0

This may not actually be required on the ghostty side. Let me do some auditing I may be able to lower this.

Raising the version would not be a deal-breaker, though. This was just the minimal version we needed until now, and we simply didn't raise it without need.

See https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/24004 (Ubuntu 20.04 is no longer available, so the only distro we'd lose is stock Debian 11.)

@mitchellh I suspect there's some misconfiguration on windows in the ghostty cmakefile. When trying to install ghostty on windows it can't find the library and errors. I can send a PR to ghostty if you'd like.

```cmake
    CMake Error at build/cmake_install.cmake:36 (file):
    file INSTALL cannot find
    "C:/Users/gocdu/programs/ghostty/zig-out/lib/ghostty-vt.dll": File exists.
```

I'd accept a PR @dundargoc but I'll see if I can fix it too. I don't have access to a windows machines so it'd be a bit of CI whack-a-mole. If you get your PR working I can add CI to make sure we don't regress it.
@dundargoc I've got this: https://github.com/ghostty-org/ghostty/pull/11756 trying to setup CI now to verify.

So one concern (discussed elsewhere) is that the repo tarball we use for dependencies contains the full Ghostty (GUI) project, including binary assets that blow up the size (>35 Mb compared to ~1 Mb for the next biggest dependency), which has significant impact on build time. This will be mitigated by stripped-down release source tarballs (thanks, @mitchellh!) for releases (e.g., https://github.com/ghostty-org/ghostty/releases/download/tip/libghostty-vt-source.tar.gz), but we can only use that for actual (future) releases since `tip` is a rolling tag, defeating the point of pinned dependencies.

This means that until we are actually integrating (a release of) libghostty, the dependency should be gated behind a flag, same as we do with WASMTIME, and tested in a single optional workflow (same as we used to do with WASMTIME before we lost interest). That should happen in this PR, after which we can merge it early in the 0.13 cycle.
